title: Johnson & Johnson — Lung — briefing Q4-2025
html:
What Changed in Lung Cancer — Q4 2025

Quarter: October–December 2025 Earnings reported: J&J January 22 | AstraZeneca February 13 (FY2025) Generated: 2026-04-27

1. TL;DR (5 Bullets)

  1. RYBREVANT FASPRO (subcutaneous) FDA approved — the single biggest structural change in Rybrevant's competitive profile; hours-long IV infusions replaced by minutes-long SubQ injection. The community oncology barrier just got lower.
  2. Rybrevant+Lazcluze hit $216M in Q4 (+76.5%) — growth rate decelerating from >100% but revenue still climbing; SubQ approval came late in the quarter so the real uptake signal comes in Q1 2026.
  3. AstraZeneca's Tagrisso surpassed $7B for FY2025 (+10%) — AZ simultaneously disclosed 8 ADCs in portfolio (3 in Phase 3), signaling they're building a Tagrisso ecosystem, not just defending a molecule.
  4. FLAURA-2 (osi+chemo) becoming the new 1L standard at AZ — AZ's earnings call positioned FLAURA-2 as "the evolution of Tagrisso," making osimertinib the combination backbone.
  5. J&J positioned Rybrevant as a key 2026 growth driver alongside TREMFYA, DARZALEX, CARVYKTI — the $50B+ aspiration by decade-end puts enormous pressure on this brand to deliver.

2. J&J Financial Signals

Metric Q4 2025 Commentary
Rybrevant+Lazcluze $216M +76.5% YoY, +9% sequential
FASPRO approval Q4 2025 SubQ — first for EGFR NSCLC
J&J total $24.6B 9.1% growth; FY2025: $94.2B (+6%)
2026 outlook Rybrevant named top growth driver $50B+ annual sales target by decade-end
  • Sequential growth modest (+9%, $198M→$216M) — SubQ approved too late in Q4 to move scripts
  • Real FASPRO test is Q1 2026 — if sequential growth accelerates to >15%, SubQ thesis is validated

What J&J management said on the Q4 2025 call:

  1. "SubQ will transform community adoption" — management framed FASPRO as a structural change to the Rybrevant franchise, not just a formulation convenience. The word "transform" signals J&J believes SubQ is the catalyst that data alone couldn't be.
  2. "We expect meaningful acceleration in H1 2026" — when analysts asked about realistic FASPRO uptake, J&J gave forward guidance pointing to H1 2026 as the proof point. This sets a specific timeline the market will hold them to.
  3. Rybrevant named as a top 2026 growth driver — alongside TREMFYA, DARZALEX, and CARVYKTI. Placing Rybrevant in this company signals internal portfolio prioritization and resource allocation commitment.
  4. $50B+ annual sales aspiration by decade-end — Rybrevant was explicitly named in the context of this long-range target, indicating the brand carries material weight in J&J's growth algorithm.

Key analyst Q&A signals:

  1. Analysts pushed on sequential deceleration (76% vs. 112%) — the growth rate question was raised directly. Management's response ("Q4 approval came late, real signal is Q1") was a deflection to the future. The Street is watching sequential growth closely as the leading indicator.
  2. "What's the realistic FASPRO uptake curve?" was asked explicitly — analysts are skeptical about how quickly community oncologists will switch from IV. J&J's answer ("meaningful acceleration in H1 2026") is a commitment that will be tested.
  3. No analyst asked about MARIPOSA OS translation to scripts — conspicuous absence. The OS publication was Q3's headline; by Q4, the Street has moved on to FASPRO as the thesis. OS data without share capture is no longer being given credit.

3. Competitor Earnings Intelligence

AstraZeneca — Tagrisso ($7.1B FY2025) + ADC Pipeline

AstraZeneca FY/Q4 2025 results (February 13, 2026):

Metric FY 2025 Q4 2025 Commentary
Tagrisso revenue $7.1B ~$1.9B +10% YoY; new FY milestone
Oncology total $25.3B — +16%; Tagrisso is 28% of AZ oncology
Dato-DXd/DATROWAY Pre-lung approval — Breast launch Mar 2025; lung Phase 3

What AZ management said on the FY2025 call:

  1. "Tagrisso continues to grow across all segments": AZ explicitly noted that Tagrisso is gaining in ADAURA (adjuvant), LAURA (unresectable Stage III), and metastatic. Their message: Rybrevant may be taking share in metastatic 1L, but Tagrisso is expanding its addressable market by moving into earlier disease stages where Rybrevant has no data. The total Tagrisso franchise is growing, not shrinking.

  2. FLAURA-2 as "the evolution": AZ management described FLAURA-2 (osimertinib + chemotherapy) as "the natural evolution of first-line treatment," positioning the combination as the default next step for Tagrisso-prescribing oncologists. The message: if you already trust osimertinib, add chemo rather than switch to a different bispecific. This directly competes with Rybrevant+Lazcluze for 1L combination therapy mindshare.

  3. ADC pipeline as the 2L ecosystem play: AZ disclosed 8 ADCs in development, 3 in Phase 3:

    • TROPION-Lung15: Dato-DXd ± Tagrisso in 2L+ EGFR NSCLC — if positive, patients who progress on 1L Tagrisso stay in the AZ ecosystem
    • TROPION-Lung14: Dato-DXd + Tagrisso in 1L NSCLC — direct 1L competitor to Rybrevant+Lazcluze
    • SAFFRON: savolitinib + Tagrisso for MET-driven resistance — explicitly targeting the mechanism Rybrevant uses (EGFR×MET bispecific)

  4. No direct mention of Rybrevant FASPRO impact: AZ did not comment on FASPRO approval in their Q4/FY results. This could mean either (a) too early to see impact, or (b) AZ doesn't view SubQ as a material competitive catalyst. If AZ addresses Rybrevant on the Q1 2026 call (April 29), that would signal they're feeling the pressure.

  5. Geographic expansion as growth lever: AZ highlighted China and emerging markets as Tagrisso growth vectors — regions where Rybrevant has minimal presence. This geographic moat protects AZ's global revenue even if US share shifts.

Implication for J&J: AZ is executing a three-layer defense: (1) Tagrisso in earlier disease stages (ADAURA/LAURA) where Rybrevant has no data, (2) Tagrisso combinations (FLAURA-2) to keep the TKI backbone familiar, (3) Tagrisso + ADC (TROPION) to own the post-progression space. J&J's counter is FASPRO convenience + OS data + multi-tumor expansion — a fundamentally different strategy (patient access vs. disease-stage depth).

Broader Lung Market Context — Q4 2025

Company Brand FY 2025 Signal
Merck Keytruda (all indications) ~$29B Patent cliff approaching 2028; Merck investing heavily in lung combos
Roche Tecentriq (lung) ~$3.5B (declining) Losing share to Keytruda; not a factor in EGFR
Daiichi Sankyo ADC platform Pipeline Multiple ADCs in lung; partnered with AZ on Dato-DXd

The broader lung market is dominated by Keytruda (~$29B across indications) with Merck's patent cliff (2028) creating a looming opportunity. For EGFR-specific competition, the landscape remains a duopoly: Rybrevant+Lazcluze vs. Tagrisso.

Synthesis: What the Combined Picture Tells Us

  1. AZ isn't defending — they're redefining: Tagrisso grew to $7.1B because AZ expanded the market (ADAURA, LAURA, geographic) while defending the core. J&J is competing against a molecule-as-platform, not a molecule-as-product.

  2. The 2L ecosystem battle will determine the long-term winner: If AZ's TROPION-Lung succeeds, patients who start on Tagrisso stay on Tagrisso + Dato-DXd when they progress. The entire treatment journey stays in AZ's hands. J&J's MARIPOSA-2 (Rybrevant in 2L) is the counter — but it's one trial vs. AZ's multi-trial 2L strategy.

  3. FASPRO is J&J's asymmetric advantage — but it's time-limited: SubQ convenience is a real differentiator NOW, but AZ will eventually develop SubQ Tagrisso or match with oral-only combinations. J&J needs to convert FASPRO's convenience advantage into installed base before AZ neutralizes it.

4. Clinical/Regulatory

  • FASPRO FDA approval (Q4 2025): First SubQ therapy for EGFR NSCLC — transformative for community adoption
  • H&N data at ESMO: Rybrevant showed signal in head & neck cancer; indication expansion pathway being built
  • MARIPOSA OS in NEJM: Published Q3; now the promotional tool for share capture
  • FLAURA-2 (AZ): osi+chemo gaining adoption; OS data pending
  • TROPION-Lung15 (AZ, Phase 3): Dato-DXd in 2L+ EGFR — the post-progression play
  • SAFFRON (AZ): savolitinib + Tagrisso for MET-driven resistance — targeting Rybrevant's mechanism

5. Thesis Update

Thesis Status Key Evidence This Quarter
1. MARIPOSA OS determines ceiling STRENGTHENED OS now published; measuring adoption response
2. Physician inertia > efficacy UNCHANGED SubQ just approved — Q1 is the test
3. AZ's ecosystem is the real moat STRENGTHENED ADAURA + LAURA + FLAURA-2 + TROPION = multi-stage defense; AZ growing Tagrisso $7.1B with no sign of competitive erosion
4. ADCs will own post-TKI space STRENGTHENED AZ: 8 ADCs, 3 Phase 3; TROPION-Lung15 enrolling; competitive pressure building
5. Community is the volume game NEEDS REVISION FASPRO addresses the convenience barrier; thesis may weaken if uptake is strong in community. Q1 data will determine.

6. What to Watch Next Quarter (Q1 2026)

  1. FASPRO uptake velocity — THE metric. Does SubQ trigger a step-change in community starts?
  2. Sequential revenue growth — $260M+ in Q1 confirms FASPRO is working; $230M suggests slow uptake
  3. AstraZeneca Q1 2026 earnings (Apr 29) — Tagrisso number, TROPION updates, any Rybrevant commentary
  4. ASCO 2026 abstracts (late May) — MARIPOSA updates, FLAURA-2 OS, HERTHENA-Lung02, TROPION
  5. ZoomRx PET/SFEA — is SubQ awareness reaching community oncologists?

7. Conversation Starters for J&J

  1. "AstraZeneca grew Tagrisso to $7.1B by expanding into adjuvant and Stage III — disease stages where you have no data. Your FASPRO advantage is real in metastatic, but AZ is building a full-journey oncology franchise. We should measure whether oncologists think of Tagrisso as 'the EGFR drug for every stage' — because that perception is AZ's real moat." — Competitor-informed strategic insight.

  2. "SubQ changes the segmentation. The question for Q1 is: are FASPRO new starts coming from oncologists who wouldn't have used IV Rybrevant (new prescribers), or from existing prescribers converting their patients? The answer determines whether SubQ is additive or just substitutive. Our PET can answer this." — Measurement value tied to the biggest product event.

  3. "AZ's TROPION-Lung and SAFFRON programs are building a 2L ecosystem around Tagrisso. If those succeed, patients who start on Tagrisso never leave AZ's portfolio. Your counter is MARIPOSA-2, but you need to win 1L FIRST to control the 2L path. FASPRO is the 1L catalyst — let's make sure we're measuring the right thing." — Connects competitor pipeline to J&J's commercial urgency.

Sources: J&J Q4 2025 earnings call (Jan 22, 2026), AstraZeneca FY/Q4 2025 (Feb 13, 2026).

Back to TA Knowledge Hub | Home
